name: write_tickets_tool
summary: Mutate the ticket queue: create new tickets, edit fields, or settle the current ticket with `done`.
Use `done` to mark the agent's current ticket finished â€” this is the only way to transition status from a tool.
Use `create` with `assign` to delegate work to another agent (their name) or scope it to a label.
constraints: Every action except `create` defaults `key` to the agent's current ticket â€” omit `key` to act on your own work.
`create` stamps `reporter` from the calling agent automatically; there is no `reporter` parameter.
`done` validates `result` against the ticket's `schema` (when set). On validation failure the call counts toward `max_schema_retries`; consecutive failures terminate ticket processing.
anti_patterns: To look up, list, or search tickets â€” use `read_tickets_tool`.
When the agent needs read AND write in the same flow â€” use `manage_tickets_tool` instead of registering both.
To delegate work to another agent â€” use `create` with `assign` set to that agent's name; the loop's Path A picks it up next iteration.
cautions: IMPORTANT: `done` is the only legal status transition from a tool. `Failed` is reserved for ticket-system-driven outcomes (schema-retry trip, policy violations).
IMPORTANT: `Done` and `Failed` are terminal â€” once settled a ticket cannot be re-opened.
ALWAYS settle your current ticket with `done` before the response ends. Returning text without `done` leaves the ticket `InProgress` and the loop will re-pick it next iteration.
output: Plain text confirmation: `Created ticket KEY`, `Edited ticket KEY`, or `Ticket KEY marked done`.
On rejection: an error describing the failed action (missing field, unknown ticket, schema-validation failure with violation list).

key.description: Ticket key (e.g. `TICKET-3`). Required for `edit` and `done` (defaults to current ticket); ignored by `create`.

task.description: For `create` (required): the task body â€” any JSON value (string, object, array, scalar). For `edit` (optional): replace the task body.

labels.description: For `create` or `edit` (optional): label scope. Determines which agents can pick up the ticket via Path B (label intersection).

assign.description: For `create` (optional): a single label OR an agent name. If it matches a registered agent, the ticket is born `InProgress` with that assignee (Path A). Otherwise it is appended to `labels` (Path B).

schema.description: For `create` or `edit` (optional): a JSON Schema document. When set, the agent's `done` result must validate against it; failures count toward `max_schema_retries`.

result.description: For `done` (optional): the agent's final answer. When the ticket carries a `schema`, this string must parse as JSON and validate. Defaults to the empty string.
